title: Slicing & Modifying ✂️
description: Chop strings apart and glue them back together perfectly.

slides.title: The Surgeon's Scalpel 🗡️
Slice Shortcuts ✂️
Replacing Text 🔄
Try It Out 💻
slides.content: Notice how slice doesn't destroy the original variable! It leaves the original intact, and RETURNS the new chopped up string to save in a new variable.

quizData.question: If you just run `myString.slice(0, 5);` by itself on a line (without assigning it to a new variable), what happens to myString?

options.text: Nothing at all. Strings are immutable.

quizData.explanation: Strings are immutable (read-only)! Slice cannot destroy the original string. It simply generates a brand NEW string and 'returns' it. If you don't save it to a variable, the new string vanishes into thin air.

slides.title: The Split Method 🪓

quizData.question: If you use `.split(" ")` (a single space character) on the string `"Hello World Today"`, what is the returned array length?
quizData.explanation: It splits the string at every space, removing the spaces entirely and isolating the words into 3 separate array items: ['Hello', 'World', 'Today'].
